channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Bulgaria's 'Bangaranga' wins Eurovision, with Israel second
item.description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Sunday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever victory in the competition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukraine launches more than 500 drones at Russia in deadly overnight attack, authorities say
item.description: Russian air defences intercepted more than 500 Ukrainian drones in a massive overnight attack that killed three people in the Moscow region, authorities said Sunday. The interceptions took place across Russia's 14 regions, as well as the annexed Crimean peninsula.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Is a Chinese invasion of Taiwan imminent?
item.description:
🇺🇸🇨🇳After nearly a decade since his last visit, US President Donald Trump is meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing in a historic summit focused on global trade, technological rivalry and rising tensions over Taiwan, one of the world’s most sensitive flashpoints.
🇹🇼China claims Taiwan as part of its territory while the United States continues to back the island with military support and strong diplomatic ties. Caught in the middle is a resilient democracy that has become one of Asia’s greatest success stories, despite never being formally recognised as a sovereign state by most other countries.
🎥FRANCE 24's Stella Elgersma takes a closer look at the tense triangle between Taiwan, China and the United States: how it came to be, what's really at stake and whether a military confrontation is truly on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Middle East live: USS Ford returns from Iran war after longest deployment since Vietnam
item.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, which was dispatched to the Middle East ahead of the outbreak of the Iran war, returned home to Virginia this weekend after a 326-day deployment, its longest since the Vietnam War. Its latest mission saw military operations in the Caribbean and the capture of Venezuela's Nicolas Maduro. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Bulgaria wins 2026 Eurovision, Israel lands a nail-biting second
item.description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest Sunday for her catchy, party anthem "Bangaranga", handing her country its first ever title. Israel's Noam Bettan looked set to win for his song "Michelle" following a big televoting score, but in the end, Bulgaria's televote put Dara at the top of a contest clouded by protests and boycotts over Israel’s participation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: FIFA says held positive talks with Iran ahead of World Cup
item.description: FIFA Secretary General Mattias Grafstrom said Saturday he held a constructive meeting with the president of Iran's FA Mehdi Taj regarding Iran’s participation in the World Cup which is set to take place this summer in the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: “Gaza Sunbirds”: Film crew arrives by sea at Cannes Film Festival
item.description: Members of "Gaza Sunbirds," a Palestinian paracycling crew formed by amputee athletes from Gaza, made a symbolic arrival by sea at the Cannes Film Festival for the screening of a documentary about them.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France monitoring hantavirus contact cases
item.description: At least one person has tested positive for hantavirus here in France and is currently in intensive care. French authorities are also monitoring four others who were onboard the MV Hondius, the cruise ship at the epicenter of the outbreak. They've also tracked 22 contact cases who've shown no signs of illness.
item.dc:creator: Catherine VIETTE

item.title: No vaccine for new highly lethal Ebola outbreak, DR Congo warns, as death toll hits 80
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in DR Congo has caused at least 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths, according to the latest figures released on Saturday. There is currently no vaccine available for the strain, known as Bundibugyo, which is considered highly lethal, the country's health minister Samuel-Roger Kamba told a press briefing in Kinshasa.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Cannes Film Festival: American movie 'Paper Tiger' makes Cannes debut
item.description: Eve Jackson, our culture editor, is live from Cannes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US and Nigeria claim killing of Islamic State second-in-command
item.description: A senior Islamic State group leader, described as "the most active terrorist in the world", has been killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces in the west African country, the two countries' presidents said.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint US-Nigerian mission
item.description: Andrew Hilliar has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK police brace far-right rally and counter demonstration in London
item.description: Thousands of people began rallying in London Saturday at a march organised by far-right activist Tommy Robinson and a counter-demonstration fused with a pro-Palestinian protest, amid a huge police presence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Congolese report constant burials as deaths in new Ebola outbreak reach 80
item.description: Dr. Gemma Nedjati Gilani, Research Associate School of Public Health - Faculty of Medicine Imperial College London, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK: Large scale far-right and pro-Palestine protests in London
item.description: Thousands of people began rallying in London Saturday at a march organised by far-right activist Tommy Robinson and a counter-demonstration fused with a pro-Palestinian protest, amid a huge police presence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Fiery Finns, Australian star favourites at boycotted Eurovision final
item.description: A fiery Finnish violinist-singer duo and an acclaimed Australian star are the favourites to win Saturday's Eurovision grand final, with the contest hit by an unprecedented boycott over Israel's participation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French actor, pop idol Patrick Bruel accused of rape by many women
item.description: French actor and pop idol Patrick Bruel is accused of rape by TV presenter Flavie Flament. The alleged assault took place when she was 16. He denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tens of thousands march in London for far-right, pro-Palestine protests
item.description: Tens of thousands of protesters marched in the UK capital on Saturday for two major demonstrations, the annual march to commemorate the Palestinian Nakba and a rally staged by British far-right activist Tommy Robinson.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Leader of Hamas's armed wing killed in strike on Gaza, says Israel
item.description: The Israeli military said on Saturday that it had killed the head of Hamas's military wing Ezzedine Al-Haddad during an air strike carried out the previous day in Gaza. Haddad's wife and daughter were also killed in the attack, according to a Hamas source.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: John Travolta feted at Cannes and receives suprise honory Palme d'Or
item.description: U.S. screen ⁠legend John Travolta, ​star of hits such as "Pulp Fiction" and "Grease," was surprised on Friday with an honorary ​Palme d'Or prize at the Cannes Film Festival, before the premiere of his directorial debut "Propeller One-Way Night Coach."
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK: Massive security operation in London as counterprotests organised
item.description: Dr. Colm Murphy, Senior Lecturer in British Politics at Queen Mary University of London, is our guest
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Scientists have identified a massive new dinosaur in Thailand
item.description: Scientists have identified a massive new dinosaur from remains discovered in Thailand, with the creature estimated to have weighed as much an nine adult elephants.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK: Far-right rally meets pro-palestine counter-protest in London
item.description: Clovis Casali is live from London.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pope Leo to visit France in September, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Leo XIV will make the first official state visit by a pontiff to France in 18 years when he travels to Paris in September, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
